beat1.title: Saving is half of the story.
body: Difficult. Necessary. The first step. But on its own, never life-changing.
$1,000 saved is $1,000 in six months. In one year. In forty years.
But that same $1,000 could be **~$2,252 in 12 years.**
Doing what every bank and every Wall Street firm has been doing for centuries. Using compound interest.
The banks know this. The funds know this. They've been using it on your money — and paying you almost nothing back.

beat2.title: The system's secret engine.
intro: You might be thinking — *how am I supposed to find money to invest?*
Don't worry. We won't lecture you about the old 50/30/20 rule. That worked when housing prices weren't skyrocketing, when inflation was under control, when groceries didn't cost an arm and a leg.
That world is gone. We live in this one.
So let's talk about something simpler. Small things you already spend on without thinking.
beat2.habitsLine: El café con tostada de cada mañana. Las cañas con amigos del finde. El streaming y el gimnasio cada mes.
beat2.vignettesIntro: If just one of those went to work instead of away — at the same 7% historical compound rate — here's what 12 years looks like:
beat2.vignettesDisclaimer: Illustrative figures based on 7% historical compound rate. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
beat2.habitsRecap: Nobody is asking you to give up your latte. Or your Friday takeout. Or your streaming. Keep what makes life good.
outro: But if even one of these — just one — went to work for you instead of disappearing every month, the math you saw above is what happens.
This is what the banks have been doing. With your money. For decades.

beat3.title: Now use it yourself.
intro: The math above used a daily latte, a Friday takeout, a monthly streaming bundle. Those are examples. Your life is yours.
So plug in your own numbers.
What do you spend on, every day or every week or every month, that you wouldn't really miss if it went to work for you instead?
Your weekly takeout. Your monthly subscription stack. The few dollars you spend on the way to work without thinking. Whatever it is — type it in below.
The chart will show you what 12 years looks like. At three different rates. Compared against what your bank is paying you right now.
wrap: This is what diBoaS is for. The part of your money you can set aside, working at a rate the system has been keeping for itself.
Your bank stays where it is — for spending, bills, and daily life. diBoaS is for money with a goal.

cta.primaryNote: Joining doesn't move your money. You decide later.

calculator.yearsTooltip: Daily uses 365 days; weekly uses 52 weeks; monthly is unchanged.
calculator.summaryHeader: In {years} years, your monthly {amount} could become…
calculator.chartAriaLabel: Compound growth chart comparing your bank rate against three scenario rates over time.
